{{Infobox book
| name = Skysworn
| slug = cradle-04-skysworn
| precedes = [[Ghostwater (novel)|Ghostwater]]
| follows = [[Blackflame (novel)|Blackflame]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-cradle-04-skysworn.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2023
| isbn = 9781959001195
| publisher = Hidden Gnome Publishing
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The story begins with [[Jai Daishou]] attempting to assassinate Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius by hiring the [[Lord of Specters]] and later trying to liberate the [[Deepwalker Ape]], both of which fail due to [[Eithan]]'s influence. While [[Lindon]] is held in confinement, [[Eithan]] and [[Yerin]] visit him to offer continued training. Meanwhile, at [[Oversight]], [[Makiel]] discusses the disappearance of Ozriel and [[Suriel]]'s interference in [[Cradle]] with [[Suriel]], noting that these events have caused a massive deviation in [[fate]]. [[Jai Daishou]]'s looting of an ancient storehouse inadvertently alerts the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] [[Dreadgod]], which begins to influence [[Yerin]]. 

As members of the [[Skysworn]], [[Renfei]] and [[Bai Rou]] attempt to reach [[Lindon]], only to find [[Eithan]] has already breached his cell to prepare him for a duel. [[Lindon]] is escorted to a mountain sanctuary where he faces [[Jai Long]] in a dispute between the Arelius family{{!}}Arelius and [[Jai]] clans. Although [[Lindon]] uses his [[Empty Palm]] technique and specialized constructs to wound [[Jai Long]], the tide turns when [[Jai Long]] manifests the [[Ancestor's Spear]]. [[Lindon]] manages to destroy the spear using his [[Blackflame madra]], but he suffers severe injuries, and during the ensuing chaos, [[Jai Long]] severs Lindon's skeletal Remnant arm{{!}}Lindon's arm. [[Eithan]] intervenes to dominate [[Jai Daishou]], and in a desperate move, [[Jai Daishou]] activates an [[Archstone]] that siphons [[madra]] from everyone present, killing himself and leaving [[Jai Chen]] empowered. 

Following the duel, [[Lindon]] is treated by the [[Soulsmith]] [[Fisher Gesha]]. [[Eithan]] provides a piece of the [[Ancestor's Spear]] to help Fisher Gesha{{!}}Gesha experiment with creating a [[Remnant]] limb for [[Lindon]]. During a journey on the cloudship [[Sky's Mercy]], [[Yerin's Blood Shadow]] awakens and attacks [[Cassias]], causing the ship to crash. A massive blood-based catastrophe begins to sweep the region as Bloodspawn{{!}}bloodspawn rise due to the awakening of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]]. Amidst this, [[Eithan]] battles an emissary from [[Redmoon Hall]], a cult serving a [[Dreadgod]], before the [[Skysworn]] [[Captain Naru Gwei]] arrives to manage the fallout. 

[[Lindon]] and his companions eventually reach [[Stormrock]], the [[Skysworn headquarters]]. There, [[Lindon]] learns of [[Redmoon Hall]]'s true nature, and [[Yerin]] expresses a desire to join the [[Skysworn]]. After a disagreement with [[Cassias]], [[Yerin]] leaves the [[Arelius family]] to join the [[Skysworn]]. [[Lindon]], seeking strength, decides to pursue his own path alongside [[Orthos]] before eventually approaching the [[Skysworn]] to join them. After being interrogated by [[Naru Gwei]] regarding [[Yerin's Blood Shadow]], [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] are allowed to apply. During the application process, [[Lindon]] finishes fourth in the [[Lowgold]] category, but he, [[Yerin]], and [[Akura Mercy]] find themselves assigned to an undersized team. 

The situation turns dire when the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] fully awakens, releasing a devastating sonic scream. [[Lindon]] rescues a wounded [[Yerin]] from [[Stormrock]], and they hide in a cellar while being hunted by Bloodspawn{{!}}bloodspawn. During this time, [[Lindon]] uses a [[binding]] technique to help [[Yerin]] gain control over her [[Blood Shadow]], allowing her to integrate it as a weapon. They witness a colossal battle between the [[Dreadgod]] and a massive armored giant. Finally, [[Eithan]] rescues the group and reveals that the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] has retreated. In exchange for information, [[Eithan]] shares a recorded message from the [[Abidan]] Ozriel regarding the nature of [[Iterations]], while the [[Arelius family elders]] formally strip [[Cassias]] of his position as Jai Patriarch{{!}}Patriarch.}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Jai Daishou]] attempts to hire the [[Lord of Specters]] to assassinate Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius, but the spirit refuses because [[Eithan]] is a friend to its flock. Seeking an alternative, [[Jai Daishou]] tries to liberate the [[Deepwalker Ape]] from a jungle prison, only to find that [[Eithan]] has already emptied it and left a mocking note. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] is visited in his confinement by [[Eithan]] and [[Yerin]], who reveal that he is being monitored rather than arrested and offer to continue his training.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Suriel]] visits [[Makiel]] at [[Oversight]], where they discuss the disappearance of Ozriel and the unintended consequences of [[Suriel]]'s interference in [[Cradle]]. [[Makiel]] reveals that both [[Suriel]]'s actions and Ozriel's descendants have caused a massive deviation in [[Cradle]]'s [[fate]], prompting him to plan an acceleration of events to force the subjects out of the world. Meanwhile, [[Jai Daishou]] successfully loots an ancient storehouse for powerful artifacts, but his presence inadvertently alerts the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] [[Dreadgod]], which begins to stir and influence [[Yerin]].}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Renfei]] and [[Bai Rou]], members of the [[Skysworn]], navigate a series of high-security magical defenses to reach their prisoner, [[Lindon]]. Upon arrival, they find the cell has been breached by Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius and his companions, who have gathered to prepare [[Lindon]] for an upcoming duel. After [[Lindon]] selects various soulsmith-made constructs for combat, the [[Skysworn]] escort him to a remote mountain sanctuary where his weapons are confiscated before he enters the arena.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|In a duel overseen by the [[Skysworn]] [[Captain Naru Gwei]], [[Lindon]] faces [[Jai Long]] to settle a dispute between the Arelius family{{!}}Arelius and [[Jai]] clans. Using a series of specialized constructs and his [[Empty Palm]] technique, [[Lindon]] manages to disrupt [[Jai Long]]'s defenses and wound him with a specialized dagger. However, the tide turns when [[Jai Long]] reveals he has reached a new stage of power by manifesting the [[Ancestor's Spear]], leaving a wounded [[Lindon]] at his mercy.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|During a duel, [[Lindon]] uses his [[Blackflame madra]] to successfully destroy the [[Ancestor's Spear]], despite suffering severe physical and spiritual injuries from [[Jai Long]]. The conflict escalates when the [[Skysworn Captain]], [[Naru Gwei]], uses a [[Ruler technique]] to restrain [[Eithan]] and the others, but [[Eithan]] breaks free with a massive pulse of [[pure madra]]. Following this disruption, [[Eithan]] turns his attention toward the Jai clan{{!}}Jai family, leaving a panicked [[Jai Daishou]] to face him alone.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|During a chaotic battle, [[Jai Long]] severs Lindon's skeletal Remnant arm{{!}}Lindon's arm while [[Eithan]] continues to dominate the [[Underlord]] [[Jai Daishou]]. In a desperate attempt to survive, [[Jai Daishou]] activates a powerful [[Archstone]] that siphons [[madra]] from everyone present, ultimately causing his own death and leaving [[Jai Chen]] overwhelmed by stolen power. As the [[Skysworn]] arrive to manage the fallout of the dangerous artifact's use, [[Eithan]] prepares to tend to a wounded [[Lindon]] and deal with the political consequences of the duel.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|After losing his right arm in a fight, [[Lindon]] is treated by the [[Soulsmith]] [[Fisher Gesha]], who presents him with three different [[Remnant]] limb options for replacement. [[Eithan]] arrives and provides the spearhead from the [[Ancestor's Spear]], allowing Fisher Gesha{{!}}Gesha to attempt an experiment by merging its [[hunger madra]] [[binding]] with the most compatible limb. During their conversation, [[Eithan]] explains that his manipulation of [[Jai Daishou]] was a calculated move to advance the [[Arelius family]] and warns [[Lindon]] about the catastrophic threat of the [[Dreadgods]].}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|While traveling on the cloudship [[Sky's Mercy]], [[Yerin's Blood Shadow]] awakens and attacks [[Cassias]], causing a chaotic struggle that leads to the ship crashing into a mountain. As [[Lindon]] and [[Eithan]] tend to the survivors, it is revealed that a massive, blood-based catastrophe is sweeping across the region. [[Eithan]] uses [[soulfire]] to forcibly remove the parasite from [[Yerin]] just as a wave of red light reaches their location.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|As Bloodspawn{{!}}bloodspawn creatures rise from spilled blood due to the awakening of the [[Bleeding Phoenix]], Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius engages in a high-speed battle with [[Longhook]], an [[Underlord]] emissary from [[Redmoon Hall]]. The conflict is interrupted by the arrival of [[Naru Gwei]], a [[Skysworn Captain]], who arrests [[Longhook]] and offers to evacuate the survivors. Amidst the chaos, [[Lindon]] encounters [[Jai Long]] and [[Jai Chen]], providing them with supplies and a safe escape route before they depart to seek refuge outside imperial territory.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Lindon]] and his companions arrive at [[Stormrock]], a massive floating city serving as the [[Skysworn headquarters]], where they are escorted by [[Renfei]] and [[Bai Rou]] to receive medical attention. During the journey, [[Lindon]] learns that he is ranked twenty-fourth among Lowgold{{!}}Lowgolds and discovers from [[Renfei]] that the group [[Redmoon Hall]] is a cult serving a [[Dreadgod]]. In private, [[Yerin]] reveals her traumatic history with a [[Blood Shadow]] and expresses a fierce desire to join the [[Skysworn]] in their fight against the cult, while [[Lindon]] urges caution and a more calculated approach.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|After a tense disagreement with [[Cassias]] regarding the costs of their training, [[Yerin]] decides to leave the [[Arelius family]] to join the [[Skysworn]], leaving a hesitant [[Lindon]] behind. Meanwhile, [[Eithan]] attends a secret meeting of The Empire{{!}}the Empire's top Underlord{{!}}Underlords where [[Emperor Naru Huan]] reveals the threat posed by [[Redmoon Hall]] and the impending awakening of a [[Dreadgod]]. The chapter concludes with [[the Emperor]] commanding [[Eithan]] to undertake a perilous mission to request aid from the Akura clan{{!}}Akura family's [[Monarch]].}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Lindon]] and [[Fisher Gesha]] successfully forge a new, white-colored [[construct]] arm using shards of the [[Ancestor's Spear]] and [[hunger madra]], though the limb initially becomes wild and uncontrollable. After [[Lindon]] manages to stabilize the arm by connecting it to his own intense desires, [[Yerin]] arrives to announce she is joining the [[Skysworn]] for a conflict against [[Redmoon Hall]]. Feeling left behind and driven by his need to grow stronger, [[Lindon]] decides to leave his current surroundings to pursue his own path alongside [[Orthos]].}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|After [[Lindon]] approaches [[Yerin]] and [[Renfei]] to join the [[Skysworn]], he is detained by [[Bai Rou]] and taken to be interrogated by the [[Underlord]] [[Naru Gwei]]. During the interrogation, [[Naru Gwei]] uses spiritual perception to discover that [[Yerin]] is hosting a [[Blood Shadow]] and suspects [[Lindon]] is part of a larger scheme by Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius. Ultimately, the [[Underlord]] decides to allow both [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] to proceed with the official application process to avoid political repercussions while placing them in harm's way.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Lindon]] and [[Orthos]] arrive at a [[Skysworn]] fairground to apply for the [[Lowgold]] competition, where [[Lindon]] is provoked by a member of the [[Kotai clan]] and responds with a forceful strike. The situation is de-escalated by [[Akura Mercy]], whose high status causes the surrounding crowd to flee in respect. Meanwhile, Eithan{{!}}Eithan Arelius travels to the [[Akura clan territory]] and receives a cryptic prophecy from [[Akura Justice]], delivered on behalf of the clan's Jai Matriarch{{!}}Matriarch.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Lindon]] completes the grueling [[Skysworn]] application process by riding [[Orthos]] through various obstacles, eventually finishing fourth in the [[Lowgold]] category. During the induction ceremony, [[Lindon]], [[Yerin]], and [[Mercy]] realize they have been intentionally assigned to a smaller, undersized team due to their controversial backgrounds. The chapter concludes with tension rising between the trainees and their [[Skysworn]] mentors just as a sudden red light plunges the world into chaos.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Cassias]] struggles to manage the displacement of [[Arelius family]] members while [[Eithan]] commands them to take risky missions south and west to gather intelligence. Amidst this tension, the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] awakens, sending a devastating sonic scream that causes chaos and physical injury across the region. [[Lindon]] and his companions are caught in the fallout, forcing him to rescue a wounded [[Yerin]] from the flying city of [[Stormrock]] and seek refuge in a cellar while being hunted by Bloodspawn{{!}}bloodspawn.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Lindon]] uses a [[binding]] technique to pull the [[Blood Shadow]] parasite away from [[Yerin]], causing it to manifest into a humanoid clone of her. After a brief struggle, [[Yerin]] manages to exert control over the [[Shadow]], successfully integrating it as a new weapon. As the group recovers, they witness a colossal battle between the [[Dreadgod]] and a massive armored giant, which [[Mercy]] identifies as her mother.}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|After hiding in a cellar for three days, [[Lindon]], [[Yerin]], and [[Mercy]] are rescued by [[Eithan]], who informs them that the [[Bleeding Phoenix]] has retreated to bide its time. In exchange for information about [[Lindon]]'s vision, [[Eithan]] shares a recorded message from Ozriel, an [[Abidan]], which reveals the vast scale of the many [[Iterations]] within [[Cradle]] and issues a call to action. Meanwhile, [[Eithan]] receives a letter from the [[Arelius family elders]] formally stripping [[Cassias]] of his position as Jai Patriarch{{!}}Patriarch.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Lindon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Yerin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Eithan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Orthos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Jai Long]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Bloodspawn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Mercy]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Naru Gwei]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Fisher Gesha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Jai Daishou]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Bai Rou]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Little Blue]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Makiel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Renfei]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Cassias]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Ozriel (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Jai Chen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Suriel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Longhook]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Yerin Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Akura Mercy]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Naru Huan|Emperor]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Redmoon Hall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Arelius family]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Blackflame Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Jai clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Stormrock]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Akura clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Empire (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Sacred Valley]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Abidan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Cradle]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Serpent's Grave]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Oversight]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Monarchs]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Starsweep Tower]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Soulsmith foundry]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Heaven and Earth Purification Wheel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Blackflame madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Goldsign]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[blood madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Burning Cloak]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[hunger madra]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Ancestor's Spear]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Thousand-Mile Cloud]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Archstone]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Remnants]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Sylvan Riverseed]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Skysworn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Blood Shadow]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Blackflame]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Dreadgod]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Bleeding Phoenix]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Lowgold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Truegold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Dreadgods]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Highgold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[sacred arts]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Underlord]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Soulsmith]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[The Way]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[dreadbeasts]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cradle
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL36391047W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
